explicit_text: I enlisted in the United States Marine Corps right after high school in July 1943. I served as a corporal and aircraft mechanic, working on fighter and transport planes during World War II. My father also served in the Marine Corps during World War I, so I was proud to follow in his footsteps. The Marine Corps shaped me, teaching me discipline and responsibility during some of the toughest times in the Pacific theater.

explicit_text: I served in World War II from 1943 to 1946, mostly in the Pacific theater. I was involved in the Battle of Okinawa and spent time at Pearl Harbor, Kwajalein, Guam, and later in Japan during the occupation. The war was a harsh and life-changing experience, but it was necessary after the attack on Pearl Harbor. It matured me and many others, and I consider it a horrible but defining event in my life.

It was an inspiring place that left a strong impression on me during my early days in the service.

explicit_text: After the Japanese surrender, I was part of the occupation forces in Japan, spending about six months there. We flew into Tokyo Bay and saw firsthand the devastation in cities like Yokohama and Tokyo, which had been flattened by B-29 firebombing. It was a sobering experience to witness the aftermath of the war and to see places like Mount Fuji and Kamakura during our stay.
